name: Izki - Route 8
name_original: Centro Btt Izki Ruta 5 – circular desde Korres
description: Explore Izki - Route 8, a moderate 7.2-mile mountain biking trail in Izki Natural Park with 827 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails in Izki Natural Park traverse a varied landscape characterized by dense beech and oak forests, limestone formations, and rolling hills. The region, part of Montaña Alavesa, offers a mix of challenging ascents and flowing descents, with routes often following ancient paths and tracks. Riders can explore diverse terrain, from wooded singletrack to wider gravel paths, providing a comprehensive mountain biking experience. Best mountain bike trails in Izki The most popular mountain bike trail is Centro Btt Izki Ruta 7 – Parque Natural de Izkiz, a 23.9 miles (38.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through the heart of the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Centro Btt Izki Ruta 5 – circular desde Korres, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.7 km) path. This circular route offers scenic views and varied terrain around Korres. Local mountain bikers also love the Las Rozas a Mojón Alto – circular desde Izki Golf, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through wooded areas and open landscapes, often completed in about 52 minutes. Mountain biking in Izki is defined by dense forests, limestone hills, and historic tracks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly rides to more advanced technical challenges. The routes in Izki are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 24 reviews. More than 795 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Izki's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I expect in Izki?
A: Izki offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, characterized by dense beech and oak forests, limestone formations, and rolling hills. You'll find a mix of challenging ascents and flowing descents, with routes often following ancient paths and tracks. The terrain varies from wooded singletrack to wider gravel paths, catering to different preferences.

Q: How many mountain bike routes are available in Izki?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike routes available in Izki, ranging from easy to difficult. This includes 6 easy routes, 41 moderate routes, and 33 difficult routes, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Izki?
A: Yes, Izki offers several easy routes suitable for families. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057581335/las-rozas-a-mojon-alto-circular-desde-izki-golf" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Rozas a Mojón Alto – circular desde Izki Golf&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 10.1 km trail leading through wooded areas and open landscapes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to see while mountain biking in Izki?
A: While exploring Izki, you can encounter several interesting landmarks and viewpoints. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5635322" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco-Navarro Railway Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2934869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Izki Gorge Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corres Village and Church&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2714569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kapildui Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2934880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soila&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Izki?
A: The mountain bike routes in Izki are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 24 reviews. Many riders praise the varied terrain, from dense forests to open landscapes, and the well-maintained paths that offer a comprehensive mountain biking experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Izki?
A: Yes, Izki features several circular routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057097486/centro-btt-izki-ruta-5-circular-desde-korres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centro Btt Izki Ruta 5 – circular desde Korres&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 11.7 km path that offers scenic views and varied terrain around Korres.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Izki?
A: The best time for outdoor activities like mountain biking in Izki, and Oman in general, is typically between October and April. During these months, temperatures are more pleasant, making for a more enjoyable riding experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in Izki?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, Izki offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco-Navarro Railway Bridge – Atauri Tunnel loop from Arabako Mendialdea&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route spans nearly 60 km and features significant elevation changes, providing a substantial challenge.

Q: What kind of natural scenery can I expect on the trails?
A: Izki's mountain bike trails offer a picturesque mix of natural beauty. You'll ride through dense beech and oak forests, past unique limestone formations, and across rolling hills. The region is known for its diverse terrain and scenic backdrops, especially with the majestic Al Hajar Mountains in the distance.

Q: Can I find routes that combine historical sites with mountain biking?
A: Absolutely. Izki is rich in history, and many routes allow you to cycle past ancient mud-brick ruins and traditional date plantations. The region is one of Oman's oldest inhabited areas, offering glimpses into its past with sites like Falaj al Malaki and the old Omani neighborhood of Harat al Nizar.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines or tunnels?
A: Yes, you can explore routes that incorporate historical railway infrastructure. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10523791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atauri Tunnel – Vasco-Navarro Railway Bridge loop from Arraia-Maeztu&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 28.7 km route that utilizes parts of the old railway, offering a unique historical cycling experience.
tips: Korres is a charming little town surrounded by mountains. Although somewhat isolated, it's not a bad place to visit for a hike, mountain bike ride, or some other fun outing.

The lookout is just off the main path coming from Korres. This trail is steep and technical in parts, but it is also a spectacular narrow trail that is fun. From the viewpoint the views are wonderful and it is worth the effort to get there.

Bujanda is a small town surrounded by mountains. At one point it had 150 inhabitants, but now it is much less. It survives thanks to its location and the fact that it attracts people looking to play sports in a beautiful natural space. There are numerous hiking and biking trails in the area.
Right off the main trail is a smaller trail that takes you up to the dam. You can see it through the trees from above on the main trail. It's a small dam but creates a nice little reservoir.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it's not overly technical, it includes noticeable climbs and elevation gains that provide a good workout. It's considered suitable for intermediate riders.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 11.66 km (7.25 miles) long and typically takes around 1 hour and 8 minutes to complete on a mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features singletrack sections with some significant climbs. You'll encounter some nice banked turns on downhill sections. Parts of the trail can be rooty, and it navigates through a woodland environment.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several interesting points. You'll go through Korres Village and Bujanda Village. There's also the Viewpoint of the Izki Ravine offering scenic views, and you'll pass by the Aranbaltza Dam.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails in Izki Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the 'Ruta 8 - Izki' trail (75%) and also intersects with parts of the 'Artzaintza naturbidea' (44%), 'Vasco-Navarroaren bide berdea' (21%), and the 'Ignatian Way' (20%).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal conditions for this route aren't detailed, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking in natural parks, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential snow/ice in winter. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Regulations for dogs in Izki Natural Park typically require them to be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check the latest park rules before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to access Izki Natural Park or this route?
faqs.answer: Izki Natural Park generally does not require specific permits or charge an entrance fee for general access to its trails. However, it's always good practice to check the official Izki Natural Park website for any updated regulations or specific activity permits that might be required.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Izki - Route 8 mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The tour data indicates the route starts circularly from Korres Village. Parking facilities are usually available in or near villages that serve as trailheads within natural parks. Look for designated parking areas in Korres.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating and noticeable climbs, this route might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. Riders should have some mountain biking experience and a reasonable fitness level. There might be easier, more family-friendly options available within Izki Natural Park.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options nearby?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Korres Village and Bujanda Village. These villages, especially Korres as a starting point, are likely to offer some basic services like cafes, pubs, or guesthouses for visitors. For more options, consider nearby larger towns.
